question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker who is isAuthorized[victim][attacker]==true call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(targetUnits, maxBuyerAssets, taker=victim, ..., collateralWithdrawals=[all victim collateral], collateralReceiver=attacker, referralFeePct=0, referralFeeRecipient=attacker) under the precondition that the victim previously called setIsAuthorized(attacker, true) on Midnight, triggering the call sequence [Midnight.isAuthorized(victim, attacker) -> true, Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, idx, assets, victim, attacker)], violating the invariant that collateral must not move without the owner's per-action consent, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure via authorization setter coarse-grant? Proof idea: fuzz (victim_collateral, attacker_authorized=true) and assert collateral balance of victim decreases while attacker receives assets without victim initiating the bundle.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker authorized for victim call repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets=1, onBehalf=victim, ..., collateralWithdrawals=[victim_full_collateral], collateralReceiver=attacker) under the precondition that victim has large collateral and minimal debt, triggering [Midnight.repay(market, 1, victim, ...) -> Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, idx, full_collateral, victim, attacker)], violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must reflect the owner's intent, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim collateral by repaying dust debt and withdrawing all collateral? Proof idea: unit test with victim.debt=1, victim.collateral=1e18, attacker authorized, assert attacker receives collateral.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker authorized for victim call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(targetUnits, minSellerAssets, taker=victim, receiver=attacker, collateralSupplies=[], takes=[buy_offers], ...) under the precondition that victim has authorized attacker and victim has existing credit, triggering [Midnight.take(offer, ..., taker=victim, receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(this)) -> sellerAssets transferred to attacker], violating the invariant that loan token proceeds must go to the position owner or their designated receiver, causing scoped impact: attacker redirects victim's sell proceeds to themselves? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter front-run a pending bundle transaction by calling setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, maxSettlementFee) between the user's transaction submission and execution, under the precondition that the user signed a bundle expecting a specific settlement fee, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee -> Midnight.take with higher buyerPrice], violating the invariant that the taker's effective price must not exceed their slippage tolerance without explicit consent, causing scoped impact: user pays more loan tokens than expected for the same units due to settlement fee increase? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget / setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter decrease settlement fee to zero for a market mid-bundle, under the precondition that a sell bundle is pending with targetSellerAssets computed at non-zero fee, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(id, idx, 0) -> Midnight.take with lower sellerPrice -> filledSellerAssets < targetFilledSellerAssets -> OutOfOffers revert], violating the invariant that a valid bundle with sufficient offers must not revert due to external fee changes, causing scoped impact: fund freeze / DoS of user's sell bundle due to fee setter manipulation? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter increase settlement fee between touchMarket and the take loop, under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits computed units at old fee but take executes at new higher fee, triggering [touchMarket snapshots old fee -> feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee -> take uses new fee -> buyerAssets per unit higher -> filledBuyerAssets overshoots targetFilledBuyerAssets -> OutOfOffers], violating the invariant that asset-targeted bundles must fill exactly targetFilledBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS or accounting mismatch between TakeAmountsLib estimate and actual take cost? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setMarketTickSpacing] Can the tickSpacingSetter decrease market tick spacing between offer signing and bundle execution, under the precondition that an offer's tick is valid at old spacing but the new spacing makes it a non-multiple, triggering [tickSpacingSetter.setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing) -> Midnight.take reverts with TickNotAccessible -> bundle catches revert and skips offer -> OutOfOffers], violating the invariant that a bundle with sufficient valid offers must complete, causing scoped impact: DoS of user bundle when tick spacing setter changes spacing invalidating queued offers? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / setMarketTickSpacing] Can the tickSpacingSetter decrease tick spacing to 1 (finest granularity), under the precondition that existing buy offers are at ticks that are multiples of old spacing, triggering [tickSpacingSetter.setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1) -> new offers at finer ticks become valid -> ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits returns non-zero for new offers -> bundle fills at worse price for seller], violating the invariant that tick spacing changes must not retroactively worsen existing position economics, causing scoped impact: seller receives fewer assets than expected because finer-tick offers at lower prices are now fillable? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / claimContinuousFee] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(market, amount=marketState.withdrawable, receiver=feeClaimer) to drain the entire withdrawable balance, under the precondition that lenders have pending credit withdrawals, triggering [feeClaimer.claimContinuousFee -> _marketState.withdrawable -= amount -> Midnight.withdraw for lender reverts with underflow], violating the invariant that lenders must always be able to withdraw their credit, causing scoped impact: fund freeze for lenders whose withdrawable is drained by feeClaimer over-claim? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized (Midnight.sol) / coarse authorization] Can an authorized operator (e.g., MidnightBundles authorized by victim) call Midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim) directly on Midnight.sol, under the precondition that victim called setIsAuthorized(MidnightBundles, true) to use the bundler, triggering [attacker calls Midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, victim) with isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true -> attacker2 gains full control over victim's Midnight state], violating the invariant that authorization grants must only be made by the account owner or their explicitly intended delegate, causing scoped impact: authorization chain escalation where bundler authorization enables attacker to grant themselves full control? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with taker=victim and collateralReceiver=attacker, under the precondition that victim authorized attacker on Midnight (isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true) and victim has collateral, triggering [isAuthorized check passes -> take fills offers for victim -> withdrawCollateral(market, idx, assets, victim, attacker)], violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal receiver must be controlled by the position owner, causing scoped impact: attacker redirects victim's collateral to themselves using coarse authorization? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee (Midnight.sol) / buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can the feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE) immediately before a lender's bundle executes, under the precondition that the lender expects a low continuous fee when buying credit units, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketContinuousFee -> Midnight.take computes buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease * MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE * timeToMaturity / WAD -> lender's pendingFee is much higher than expected], violating the invariant that lenders must be able to predict their continuous fee obligation at the time of credit purchase, causing scoped impact: lender's effective yield is reduced by unexpected continuous fee increase applied at take time? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / referralFeePct near WAD] Can an attacker acting as msg.sender (authorized for taker) pass referralFeePct = WAD - 1 and referralFeeRecipient = attacker, under the precondition that taker has authorized attacker and maxBuyerAssets is large, triggering [pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets) -> referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets * (WAD-1) / 1 -> nearly all filledBuyerAssets sent to attacker -> refund = maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets ≈ 0], violating the invariant that referral fees must not exceed a reasonable fraction of the transaction value, causing scoped impact: attacker drains nearly all of taker's loan tokens via near-maximum referral fee? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized chain] Can an attacker who is authorized for victim use Midnight.multicall to call setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, victim) and then use attacker2 to call the bundle, under the precondition that victim authorized attacker (isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true) and attacker calls Midnight.multicall([setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, victim)]), triggering [attacker.multicall -> setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, victim) passes because isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true -> attacker2 now authorized -> attacker2 calls bundle with taker=victim], violating the invariant that authorization delegation must not create unbounded authorization chains, causing scoped impact: authorization chain escalation enabling attacker to create sub-delegates with full victim control? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized not scoped by market] Can an attacker authorized for victim on market A use the bundle to withdraw victim's collateral from market B, under the precondition that authorization is not scoped by market (isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true grants access to all markets), triggering [attacker calls bundle with takes from market A but collateralWithdrawals from market B using takes[0].offer.market], violating the invariant that authorization for one market must not grant access to positions in other markets, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's collateral from unintended markets using coarse authorization?
